---
title: "Week 9 Milestone Tables"
author: "<img src='images/logo.png' width='50' /> <br> Dairy Cow Foot Doc"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    theme: flatly
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
  echo: false
embed-resources: true
params:
  event:
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  broom,
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  survival,
  survminer,
  ggsurvfit,
  flextable,
  gtsummary,
  marginaleffects,
  scales,
  zoo
  )

events_formatted <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_formatted.parquet') 

animals <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/animals.parquet')

deno_cal <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/denominator_by_calendar_time_period.parquet') |>
  filter(deno_type == "lact_basic" &
           calendar_time_period_type == "year") |>
  select(-c(contains("dop"), day_of_phase_group))

  
# load GC's functions
source('../functions/fxn_load_os_fxns.R')
fxn_load_os_fxns()

# Load Parnell CR fucntions from Github
cr_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ParnellPharma/ParnellFunctionsPublic/main/parnell_calculate_CR_master.R"
source(cr_url)

# create variables for inline code
farm_name <- unique(events_formatted$location_event)

date_max_pull <- max(events_formatted$date_event, na.rm = TRUE)
date_min_pull <- date_max_pull - years(1)

# pull solution data

# summary(animals)
# glimpse(animals)
# summary(events_formatted)
# glimpse(events_formatted)
# unique(events_formatted$event_type)
# unique(events_formatted$event)


```


# Report for `r farm_name[[1]]` and `r farm_name[[2]]`

## Objective

Run this chuck below. It will open the example report in your browser.
You should delete this chunk in your final report

```{r}
#| label: open-html-r
#| eval: false

# 1. Define the file path
file_path <- "images/milestone_09.htm"

# 2. Open the browser
browseURL(file_path)

```


The objective of this milestone is start working with multiple herds, create tables, use the denominator data and use external functions. The data and functions you need are loaded in the top chunk. This is in prep for milestone 10 where we will use parameters to run the same report for multiple herds.

The steps for this milestone are as follows (code sections for these steps are below)

1.  Re-Run **step0_master_processing.R**
2.  Describe the herd size for the past 5 years for the 2 herds
3.  Run the parnell CR function, by calling **fxn_create_CR_reports**
    1.  More details about the function can be found [here](https://github.com/ParnellPharma/ParnellFunctionsPublic/blob/main/parnell_calculate_CR_master.R)
4.  Run the **fxn_dz_status** as it relates to mastitis to determine if type early mastitis case influences conception risk.
5.  Create tables from the inventory and both conception risk data frames.

## Describe Herds

-   Using the deno_cal dataframe recreate this table using the gt table skills you learned this week. You will probably need to look at the package website or use AI to get some of the details.

-   Some gt function hints are:

    -   groupname_col and rowname_col inside gt()

    -   fmt_data

    -   fmt_number

    -   tab_style

    -   cols_label

    -   tab_spanner

    -   tab_options

```{r}
#| label: describe-herds

# =========================================================================
# Initial data summary of deno_cal
# =========================================================================

library(dplyr)

deno_summary <- deno_cal %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(date_time_period_start),
    `Lactation Group` = dplyr::case_when(
      `Lactation Group` == "LACT > 0" ~ "Adults",
      TRUE ~ `Lactation Group`
    )
  ) %>%
  # Grouping order
  # Lactation Group column name contains a space, so it must be referenced with backticks
  group_by(
    year,
    `Lactation Group`,
    location_lact_list
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    ct_animal_days_elig   = sum(ct_animal_days_elig, na.rm = TRUE),
    ct_animals            = sum(ct_animals, na.rm = TRUE),
    ct_animal_lactations  = sum(ct_animal_lactations, na.rm = TRUE),
    days_in_time_period   = sum(days_in_time_period, na.rm = TRUE),
    ct_animal_time_periods = sum(ct_animal_time_periods, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# deno_summary

# =========================================================================
# Apply gt table formatting of summarized data
# =========================================================================

# Add first/last row markers
deno_summary <- deno_summary %>%
  dplyr::group_by(year) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    first_row = dplyr::row_number() == 1,
    last_row  = dplyr::row_number() == dplyr::n()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# Build gt table
deno_summary_gt <- deno_summary %>%
  gt(
    groupname_col = "year",
    rowname_col   = "Lactation Group"
  ) %>%
  
  # Hide helper columns
  cols_hide(columns = c(first_row, last_row)) %>%
  
  # Label columns
  cols_label(
    year                    = "Year",
    location_lact_list      = "Herd",
    ct_animal_days_elig     = "Animal Days Eligible",
    ct_animals              = "Animals",
    ct_animal_lactations    = "Animal Lactations",
    days_in_time_period     = "Days in Period",
    ct_animal_time_periods  = "Animal Time Periods"
  ) %>%
  
  # Add spanners for logical grouping
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Counts",
    columns = c(ct_animals, ct_animal_lactations)
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Time-Based Metrics",
    columns = c(ct_animal_days_elig, days_in_time_period, ct_animal_time_periods)
  ) %>%
  
  # Format numbers with commas
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(
      ct_animal_days_elig,
      ct_animals,
      ct_animal_lactations,
      days_in_time_period,
      ct_animal_time_periods
    ),
    decimals = 0
  ) %>%
  
  # Bold column labels
  tab_style(
    style = list(cell_text(weight = "bold")),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  # Italic year group labels
  tab_style(
    style = list(cell_text(style = "italic")),
    locations = cells_row_groups()
  ) %>%
  
  # Bold the Lactation Group column values
  tab_style(
    style = list(cell_text(weight = "bold")),
    locations = cells_body(columns = "Lactation Group")
  ) %>%
  
  # Shade Herd 2755f rows
  tab_style(
    style = list(cell_fill(color = "lightblue")),
    locations = cells_body(rows = location_lact_list == "Herd 2755f")
  ) %>%
  
  # Bold box around YEAR label row
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = "all",
      color = "gray",
      weight = px(3)
    ),
    locations = cells_row_groups()
  ) %>%
  
  # Bold TOP border for first row of each year
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = "top",
      color = "gray",
      weight = px(3)
    ),
    locations = cells_body(rows = first_row)
  ) %>%
  
  # Bold BOTTOM border for last row of each year
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = "bottom",
      color = "gray",
      weight = px(3)
    ),
    locations = cells_body(rows = last_row)
  ) %>%
  
  # Global table options
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(14),
    row_group.as_column = TRUE,
    data_row.padding = px(6)
  )

deno_summary_gt





```

## Conception Risk Tables

Use the Parnell CR function to compare the conception risks between the 2 herds and recreate this table

1.  The **fxn_create_CR_reports** function will work if you just pipe the events_formatted dataframe to it.
2.  You will need define the grp_vars inside the function

-   the last one should be Rstandard
-   the table uses lact_group_basic as one of the variables to group the data

3.  for the table use some of the same gt function you used in describing the herd sizes.

-   additional functions to explore are:
-   summary_rows
-   cols_nanoplot \_ cols_merge_range

```{r}
#| label: create-cr_table

## Explore the data
# unique(events_formatted$location_event)
# explore column names
# colnames(events_formatted)

# Add floor_date_month in events_formatted
events_formatted <- events_formatted %>%
  mutate(floor_date_month = floor_date(date_event, unit = "month")) %>%
  mutate(floor_date_year = floor_date(date_event, unit = "year"))

glimpse(events_formatted)

# Pipe events_formatted into the CR report function to create "cr_report"
cr_report<-events_formatted%>%
           fxn_create_CR_reports(grp_vars = c('location_event', 'lact_group_basic', 'floor_date_year', 'Rstandard'))

# cr_report
#   write_csv(cr_report,"excel_excuses.csv")

library(dplyr)
library(gt)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(scales)

# ---- PREP DATA ----
cr_clean <- cr_report %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(floor_date_year),
    Lactation = case_when(
      lact_group_basic == "LACT > 0" ~ "Adults",
      TRUE ~ lact_group_basic
    ),
    `95% CI` = sprintf("%.1f-%.1f", CI_lower, CI_upper)
  ) %>%
  arrange(location_event, lact_group_basic, year)

# ---- FUNCTION TO BUILD ONE TABLE ----
build_cr_table <- function(df) {
  
  subtitle_value <- unique(df$location_event)
  
  # Create a Unicode bar representation for CR
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      CR_scaled = scales::rescale(CR, to = c(1, 20)),
      CR_bar = strrep("█", round(CR_scaled))
    )
  
  df %>%
    select(
      year,
      Lactation,
      Event = `Not Bred Event`,
      Preg = preg,
      Total = total,
      SPC,
      Risk = CR,
      `95% CI`,
      CR_bar
    ) %>%
    gt(rowname_col = "year") %>%
    
    # Title row labels
    cols_label(
      Lactation = "Lactation",
      Event = "Event",
      Preg = "Preg",
      Total = "Total",
      SPC = "SPC",
      Risk = "Risk",
      `95% CI` = "95% CI",
      CR_bar = " "
    ) %>%
    
    # Subtitle row (location_event)
    tab_header(
      title = "",
      subtitle = subtitle_value
    ) %>%
    
    # Conception % spanner
    tab_spanner(
      label = "Conception %",
      columns = c(SPC, Risk, `95% CI`)
    ) %>%
    
    # Formatting
    fmt_number(
      columns = c(Event, Preg, Total),
      decimals = 0,
      use_seps = TRUE
    ) %>%
    fmt_number(
      columns = c(SPC, Risk),
      decimals = 1
    ) %>%
    
    # Style the bar column
    tab_style(
      style = cell_text(color = "orange", weight = "bold"),
      locations = cells_body(columns = CR_bar)
    ) %>%
    
    # Alternating row shading
    tab_options(
      row.striping.background_color = "#f2f2f2",
      row.striping.include_table_body = TRUE
    ) %>%
    
    # Summary row
    summary_rows(
      groups = NULL,
      columns = c(Risk),
      fns = list(`Location Average` = ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
      formatter = fmt_number,
      decimals = 1
    ) %>%
    
    # Style summary row
    tab_style(
      style = list(
        cell_fill(color = "#d9e6f2"),
        cell_text(weight = "bold")
      ),
      locations = cells_summary()
    )
}

# ---- BUILD FOUR TABLES ----
tables <- cr_clean %>%
  group_split(location_event, lact_group_basic) %>%
  map(build_cr_table)

# Access tables as:
# tables[[1]]
# tables[[2]]
# tables[[3]]
# tables[[4]]




```

## Mastitis and Conception Risk

Now recreate the table low were a variable was created to determine if a cow had mastitis in her first 60 DIM.

1.  Once you create the new mastitis variable then run the **fxn_dz_status**

    -   The gap should be 14 days

    -   If the cow had mastitis in the first 60 DIM then she will be classified as

        -   "New" if it was her first case

        -   "Repeat" if it was a subsequent case within the gap

        -   "Chronic" if she had at least 1 case after the gap

2.  After running this function

    -   assign cows that had mastitis \>60 DIM as "After"

3.  Merge the data back with events formatted.

    -   I have provided some code below to help you get started.

    -   After the join, cows with no mastitis events should be coded as No Mast

4.  After you merged the mastitis status variable back into events_formatted run the **fxn_create_CR_reports** function again to get the conception risk by mastitis status

5.  Then create a table to show the results

```{r}
#| label: mast


# code to help you
# run this after you've run fxn_disease_status and before you join back to events_formatted

# your code that runs fxn_dz_status should create this data frame
select(id_animal_lact = id_animal, location_event, lact_number,
         mast_60, life_times_mast_60,
         status_mast_60) |>
  group_by(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number, mast_60) |>
  # get last mast mast_60 just using life time ignores mast_60
  slice_max(order_by = life_times_mast_60, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  # now will only end up with 1 row/cow
  group_by(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number) |>
  slice_max(order_by = mast_60, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup()

# then join back to events_formatted
 



```

# Extension

For this week extension continue to create standard things you would want to report or incorporate modeling techniques to explore the data

Ideas

-   Try the code with other diseases like mastitis or culture information
